Okay, now I'm going to do a video on the new keyword planner that replaced the Google AdWords keyword tool. I'm just going to go through some of the differences and also how to use the new keyword planner. First thing I want to talk about are the negatives. The first negative is that you have to log into an AdWords account to access the planner. Now it's always been like that, so this is no new thing. Now this time the planner has no match type data for search volume. The old keyword tool, you could search on phrase match, broad match, and exact match, but now you're pretty much limited to exact match in the keyword tool or the planner itself. One of the major negatives is that the planner has no match type data for search volume. As you know with the earlier Google keyword tool, you're able to search for exact match, broad match, and phrase match, but now they've pretty much eliminated the broad and phrase and they only have the exact match. The broad and phrase matches have disappeared in the regular searches, but you can still actually access them when you look at the Google AdWords ad groups and we'll go into that later. Another thing is that there's no device targeting. Back in the day you were able to target by default the desktop and laptop computers, and then you could also search for mobile and tablet, but now they've pretty much consolidated everything, so you can't really target a specific device. There's also no local versus global monthly searches. Now in the old keyword tool, you could do a basic search and you would see the global searches on one column and the local monthly searches in another column, but now you can't do that, but you can target geographically more specifically, which is a good thing, and we'll talk about that later. Now let's talk about the positives. Positives include you have the ability to segment by geography, and you can dig really, really deep into the city level to get keyword search volume data. Now remember back in the day you weren't be able to do that. You could do that with something like Google Trends, but now you have a centralized location where you can do it with the Google Keyword Planner, so that's definitely positive, especially if you're a local business, somebody trying to do local searches. When you do a search and you go to the keyword planner main page, you'll be presented with four different types of searches, and I'll show you that more depth in the next video, but you do a regular keyword search, which will basically give you the keyword ideas along with the ad group ideas for PPC. This is kind of similar to what they did before, and you can do bulk uploading, which means if you have a huge list of keywords, you can just upload it, and the keyword planner will get you the performance data that you want. Another positive is that the planner will also search volumes by ad group, landing page, and other custom categories that you can set up. Users can also multiply keywords, which is kind of a strange thing. I'll show you more about that later. Basically what you do is you enter keywords on the left-hand side, and then keywords on the right-hand side, and then it automatically combines them together. We're going to go ahead to the next video segment now. We're going to move on to the four different types of searches. I'm going to show you exactly what that looks like so that you know what you have to do, what you have to change in order to adapt to the new Google Keyword Planner. Now that you understand the four different routes that you can take, let's go under the cover of the Google Keyword Planner tool and take a look at some keyword search examples. I'm going to walk you through step-by-step and give you a good overview of this tool. When you sign into your Google AdWords account, there's two ways to actually get to this keyword tool. If you're not in Google AdWords, you can go search keyword planner, and then just click on the first listing and you'll get keyword tool, and then it will ask you to log into your AdWords account. If you're already in your AdWords account, when you log in, you get here to your home page, and then all you do is go over here on tools and keyword planner is right there. So you just click there, and then you'll get to the home screen for the Google Keyword Planner, which is this. So here are the four searches that you can have. You can have search for new keyword and ad group ideas, which is basically what we did with the old keyword tool. That didn't do ad group ideas, it only did keyword ideas, but that's this search. And then this is if you want to upload a list of keywords, you can upload at least 10,000 of them, and then you can get search volume or group them into ad groups. And then you can also get traffic forecasts for a list of keywords, which would be the same list here, it's just two different searches. And then here is the multiplier, which we'll get into in a little while. So first we're going to start with this new keyword, search for new keyword and ad group ideas. And basically all you do is you enter your keyword, I'm going to enter dog training, and then if you want you can enter a landing page or a category, I'm going to leave that for now. And now down here you get your targeting, you can target everywhere. I'm just going to target the United States. And then you can do a location within the United States, like say I want to do, say I'm working for somebody in New York, I could target New York, New York if I wanted to. But right now I'm just going to target the whole United States, so I'm going to remove that. And so now you can do English, you can do whatever language, all kinds of different languages, I'm going to do English. And then you can have Google or Google and search partners, we're just going to do Google. And then negative keywords. I'm going to put in free and cheap. So we're going to do everything but nothing free and nothing cheap. And date range, the average monthly searches for the last 12 months, you can do any date range that you want. Keyword filters, you can get only keywords that have a certain number of monthly searches. So let's say I'll put in 100 here. And if you want greater than equal, less than or equal to a suggested bid, I'm going to leave that out because I'm not doing ad words right now. And add, impression, share, the same thing. And I'm going to use, I'm going to select all types of competition. And then you just click on get ideas. Okay, here we are. So this shows the average trend for dog training. Now it looks like it's fairly consistent, jump in January, because I guess people are doing their New Year's resolutions. And then when is this? July, I guess during the summertime, it's popular as well. But it's fairly consistent. You don't see a huge seasonal nature here with dog training. Now, if we move down, you see that the first thing you get is ad group ideas, because Google obviously is trying to get you to use Google AdWords. So they suggest all these ad groups. We're going to start with keyword ideas. So you just click over here, get keyword ideas. And so we have dog training. These are all the keywords that they've come up with. If you scroll down all the way, there's a total of 609 altogether. Now, this is obviously the keyword, average monthly searches. Now remember, this is on all devices and competition. This is not competition for SEO. This is competition for ads for Google ads. Suggested bid 750, 857. So these are fairly pricey keywords. Over here, say you select one. Over here, you can add it to your plan. And it gives you a bid range, $0 to $30. Okay, what happens with this is the bid range is here, 0 to 44. You say, I'm not going to do 44. I'm going to do like $5.92. It's going to be my maximum bid. So they say that for this $5.92, you're going to get about 47 clicks and you'll spend about $147 for the day. And we have one ad group right here. We haven't added in any. You can add in an ad group if you want, say dog training school. See, I want to add. Okay, so now I have dog training school here as my second ad group. And it doesn't have, this is the number of keywords that are in it. See, I had already took this dog training San Diego and added it to dog training. I haven't added anything to dog training school, so that shows no ads in there. So that's all I really want to talk to about. That's all I really want to mention about that. You can always change your search. This is the criteria that you had entered before. You can always change that there. Now let's go to the, oh, and I think I said, if you want to add something, you just click on this add to plan. See, it's adding there. If you want to add something here, ABC dog training is a, it's actually a school for dog trainers. So we'll do that dog training school. And so now we have one keyword in that one, which is dog training. And in this dog training, we have two. So those are, that's how you set up your ad groups. If you want to do ad words. Okay. So that's about it. You can download your keywords and you could add them all to a plan and you download them. You would download them. Well, this is an Excel file. If you, you could use AdWords, if you're using the AdWords editor, you can do that. But normally you would do an Excel file, but I'm not going to download them. Oh, I forgot one thing. The search chart for, what is it, the trends, the number of searches, monthly searches, just shows you for each keyword. So I guess in the winter, online dog training is big and dog training methods kind of kind of consistent all the time. Canine dog training kind of goes up in the spring and then it goes back up in the fall. Dog training tricks are big in November, January, which is of course New Year's resolutions. Everybody wants their dog to be the best dog. And dog training Austin is bigger here. So that's, that's that. I'm not going to belabor that anymore. I guess that's about it for this. Then you go to the ad groups and they have suggested ad groups. They've already taken these 600 keywords and put them into ad groups for you. Click on that and you get all the keywords that relate to that. It might be a good way to sort them actually if you're, because you have 600 keywords, you want to want to sort them into, look for ones that are actually useful. And then you can take individual, then you have the same kind of thing here as you had before and you can take them, these individual ones and add them to your, add them to your ad groups here. You click on add all, then what happens is this whole ad group moves over here. So you have a new ad group. So it makes it a lot easier if you're doing, if you're doing Google AdWords. I think that for that search, that's about it. So let's go back here. Let's go back here. I'm going to say start over because I want to go back to the original menu. And so next we're going to do is get search volume for a list of keywords. And what you do here, you just enter your keywords. I don't have a list. That's enter your keywords. You can either, you have like, if you have them like in a notepad or something, you could always enter them like that. And if you have a file, you just browse to your computer and get the file there. And then we have the same targeting here. And so now we want to do, because we're going to do the dog training and dog training school in the United States. And we're going to take out these keyword. Well, it's not a search. So anyway, click on that and you get the actual search, get the search volume. And so there's our two keywords. And so now we have the, so these are for specific keywords. You don't want to just do a search. You want to add in your own keywords. And so then everything else after that is pretty much the same. So let's go back and close that up. Okay, here, if you click here, you get a traffic, get the traffic forecast for a list of your keywords. And you can do the match for this traffic, the traffic amounts. So say we have quotation marks for phrase match and we have the square brackets for exact match. So why don't we go dog training? You could also upload a file if you want to. And then we get down here, the targeting, we're going to leave the targeting the same and get forecasts. Okay, now in order to do the forecast, we have to enter a bid amount. So say we bid $5. Click on that and this is $5 right here. Now let's do a daily budget, say $10. You can play with this on your own. It's, you know, this is just basically what it does. And that's not going to work because this is a limited budget. Oh, you know why? Because my, let me do $100 a day. So I get a little bit, not a lot. What you expect to get for $100 a day is 44 clicks, 1,000 impressions, costs $100, average cost per click, $2.27, average position between positions 1 and 2. So that's that. That's the traffic.
